subject: Computer Networking
title: Role-Based Access Control
description: A fast breakdown of Role-Based Access Control for builders.

hook.text: Should every employee access every file? Of course not. RBAC ensures people only access what their role requires.

buildup.text: Roles group permissions. An 'editor' can write content. A 'viewer' can only read. An 'admin' can manage users.

discovery.text: Users get roles, not individual permissions. Adding a new hire means assigning one role, not fifty rules.

twist.text: Role explosion is real. Too many fine-grained roles become as unmanageable as individual permissions.

climax.text: Principle of least privilege: give the minimum access needed. If a role doesn't need it, remove the permission.

punchline.text: RBAC matches access to responsibility. Nothing more.

quiz.question: What principle should guide RBAC design?
options: Least privilege — grant only the minimum access needed
Maximum access — let everyone see everything
Equal access — give all users identical permissions
